channel.title: Posts on Stori Wyllt 2026
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Stori Wyllt 2026

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi and wildlife partnership work in Cardigan
item.description:
The Grassland ID workshops, organised by Ceredigion Nature Partnership in 2025 were a great success, and showed a fantastic working partnership between the Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi, Friends of Netpool Park, West Wales Biodiversity Information Centre and the Partnership.

What has inspired Stori Wyllt in addition to the great work at Netpool Park has been the collaboration between the school, Wales & West Housing Assocation and Cardished to create wildlife habitat with residents and students. WWHA received a Small Grant from the Partnership for wildlife enhancements across their Ceredigion properties, and this included the creation of bug hotels with the students from Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi and the residents of Llys Owen, with a workshop run by Harry from Cardished.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 1
item.description: Oriel Gallery

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 1

item.title: Mishaps & magic at Esgair Arth
item.description:
After the first workshop for Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron in the morning, I returned back up to a potential site just north of Aberaeron and inland from Aberarth, on the grounds of a farm called Esgair Arth. It is the old house of a famous local witch called Mari Berllan Peter, about whom our artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly has done so much work already.


Mari Berllan Peter‘s old house by the Afon Arth

item.title: Workshop No.1 at Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron
item.description: Oriel Gallery

item.title: Tŵr Gwyllt at Aberaeron
item.description:
Guest post by artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly:

While Jake was visiting the Jewsons yard with Ysgol Aberaeron, we came across this toad tucked beneath a log, a quiet little resident already making use of the space.

I’m really hoping he might take shelter in one of the Lletau Llyffant I’ve created; these are small shelters made with the toads in mind.

A little about Llyffantod

In old Welsh folk belief, toads were linked to wise women and hidden knowledge, and were said to carry carreg y llyffant in their heads, a magical stone with protective powers. Living between water and land, tucked beneath logs and stones, they’re natural threshold keepers, creatures of the in-between, brushing right up against the Otherworld of Annwn.

item.title: Aberaeron site reccie
item.description:
Last year, we ran the Grassland ID workshops at Penmorfa with Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron.

This year, we’re hoping for another magical spot, with a witch’s house, but this has yet to be confirmed. I had a chance meeting with my colleague, Tree Officer Norman Birch, and he explained that there is a public foot path in Aberaeron that runs on the south bank of the river Aeron, past the allotments an up to Jewsons.

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar site visit
item.description:
A foundational element of the Stori Wyllt project has been to foster strong relationships between the schools, creatives and naturalists. This is why the initial Stori Wyllt Symposiwm was so important; an opportunity for practitioners to meet each other and ask questions.

On a gloriously sunny Thursday afternoon, I met up with ecologist Sheena Duller, artist Marian Haf and headteacher at Ysgol Llanarth, Jannette Jones.

We walked first past the village pond

item.title: Stori Wyllt Symposiwm
item.description:
Croeso i’r Stori Wyllt symposiwm!
Welcome to the Stori Wyllt symposium!

The Stori Wyllt Symposiwm was an ideal opportunity for the teachers, naturalists and creatives from the four schools to meet each other in-person and share ideas at a central location in Ceredigion (Moody Meadows at Bargoed Farm ar bwys Llwyncelyn).

The Stori Wyllt schools are, from the south to the north, Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi, Ysgol Llanarth, Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron and Ysgol Llanilar.
